metric_value: Internet Object Computing: Java + CORBA + ISP services enabling distributed enterprise computing across corporate boundaries
notes: Aberdeen's coined analytical framework for the practice area.

metric_value: Overall Java market will be significant; market share cannot yet be predicted due to bifurcation caused by Microsoft's alternative approach
notes: Key finding #2; prescient about Java's eventual mainstream adoption.

metric_value: Most if not all proprietary directories headed for extinction as LDAP becomes entrenched; even LDAP-capable proprietary directories will need to become fully interchangeable
notes: Key finding #4; highly prescient — LDAP became the industry standard.

metric_value: Deferred verification of LDAP dominance prediction.
notes: Deferred verification of LDAP dominance prediction.

metric_value: EJB extremely immature but rare supplier alliance forming; Microsoft has large installed base of COM-based apps; developer community clearly favors Microsoft in 1998
notes: Key finding #7; Microsoft developer dominance accurately captured.

metric_value: Windows DNA not made for inter-enterprise computing; NT-tied; lacks scalability and platform independence; Microsoft-only strategy risks missing IBM/Netscape/Sun/Oracle benefits
notes: Aberdeen critical of Microsoft's Internet Infrastructure strategy; assessment proved accurate as DNA was replaced by .NET.

metric_value: Key watch area: Microsoft delivery of production-quality COM+ on Unix platforms will greatly extend interoperability story
notes: Prediction: COM+ never gained meaningful Unix traction; Windows/.NET won on Microsoft terms.

metric_value: Deferred verification.
notes: Deferred verification.

metric_value: Consumers driving market rejection of push and publish/subscribe; e-mail underestimated and outperforms complex messaging alternatives
notes: Key finding #3; highly accurate — push technology faded rapidly after 1999.

metric_value: Role of Internet outsourcers will increase substantially over next 5 years; migration from proprietary systems; software/hardware costs insignificant vs. expert labor
notes: Key finding #6; highly prescient — ISP/managed hosting/cloud services boomed.

metric_value: Deferred verification.
notes: Deferred verification.

metric_value: Porting current applications to Java rarely satisfactory; Java is a communications environment not just a language; applications must be rewritten
notes: Key finding #10; important practical limitation of Java adoption.

metric_value: Three barriers to enterprise-class Internet deployment: Security, Trust, and Reliability; trust requires directory-based security certificates and PKI
notes: Key finding #13.

metric_name: EJB vs COM+ runtime battle outcome prediction
metric_value: Java alliance expects EJB runtime on multiple platforms; Microsoft expects NT+COM+ on multiple hardware; battle will fragment market over next 1-2 years
notes: Key finding #14; highly prescient about the years of fragmentation.

metric_value: Excellent technology; struggles with consistent message and marketing strategy; many strategic relationships via Visigenic; unable to leverage into lasting market position
notes: Aberdeen assessment of Inprise/Borland.

metric_value: Domain FoundationTools and FoundationWare appeal to large-enterprise; 'honest broker'; LDAP engine from Netscape as differentiator; never first to market; challenge: gain mindshare from IBM
notes: HP's strategic positioning in the Internet Infrastructure market.

metric_value: Exceptionally attractive solution; popular offerings across all IOC components; clear vision; mindshare in e-commerce; challenges: integrate disparate offerings and demonstrate enterprise-scale deployment
notes: Aberdeen most positive on IBM of all vendors reviewed.

metric_value: Led on open standards; SuiteSpot initially defined Internet computing; too slow on server-side Java; lost momentum to Microsoft and IBM; new NetCenter + IOC Business Services strategy announced
notes: Aberdeen notes Netscape's business model converging with IBM's — differentiation problem.

metric_value: IntranetWare maintaining NOS dominance; EJB as infrastructure strategy; must prevent NT 5.0 erosion; Eric Schmidt-led Java/Internet plan; execution remains to be seen
notes: Key risk: NT 5.0 (Windows 2000) threat to NetWare installed base — proved accurate.

metric_value: NCA strategy: Oracle8 + Java + Designer/2000 + Developer/2000; e-commerce providers report Oracle's products too proprietary and less useful than Microsoft's in Internet implementations
notes: Notable negative customer feedback cited.

metric_value: Substantial Java mindshare; Certificate, Mail and Directory Servers offered as components not integrated; need to integrate development tools/middleware/Java into scaleable offering
notes: Sun's fragmentation of its own Java ecosystem noted.

metric_value: Adaptive Server + Jaguar CTS + PowerDynamo + Enterprise CONNECT + Powersoft = integrated platform; challenge: Powersoft perceived as proprietary; market unaware of Sybase middleware
notes: Heritage perception problem accurately identified.

metric_value: CORBA extends Java capabilities to legacy and disparate platforms; Aberdeen predicts CORBA will become lingua franca between different Java environments
notes: Prediction was partially correct; CORBA use declined as REST/web services emerged.

metric_value: Look for: in-depth knowledge of installed environment + proof of interoperability; AND credibility in supporting all relevant Internet Infrastructure services + consulting best-practices expertise
notes: Practical buyer guidance for IS managers.
